title: Common Mitigation Enumeration (CME) Entry
description: A structured defensive control entry in the CME taxonomy, designed to complement CVE/CWE by identifying mitigations and mapping their deterministic impact on CVSS environmental scoring.

control_name.description: Formal name of the mitigation control

description.description: Technical description of what the control does and how it mitigates risk

category.description: Sub-category within the tactic (e.g., Kernel Hardening, Network Isolation)

category_id.description: Kebab-case identifier linking to the category registry in data/categories.json

control_layer.description: Technology stack layer where the control resides

cvss_vector_impacts.description: One or more CVSS base metric modifications this control provides

cwe_relationships.description: CWE weakness classes this control mitigates

method.description: Human-readable description of how to verify the control is active

expected.description: Expected output or pattern indicating the control is active

verification.description: How a scanner or agent can confirm the control is active

d3fend_mapping.description: Mapping to equivalent D3FEND technique if one exists

references.description: External references (Red Hat docs, NIST, CIS benchmarks, etc.)

confidence.description: Confidence level that the CVSS attenuation is deterministic and verifiable

platforms.description: Legacy platform applicability list. Use cve_affected for structured product identity with CPE/PURL support.

cve_schema_version.description: CVE Record Format schema version that cve_affected conforms to (e.g., '5.1', '5.2.0'). Pinned explicitly so parsers don't have to detect it. Required when cve_affected is present.

cve_affected.description: Products and platforms where this control is applicable, using the CVE 5.x affected[] container shape. Enables structural joins against CVE records for product-scoped mitigation queries.

from.description: Original metric value (e.g., N, L, H, C)

to.description: Modified metric value when control is active

rationale.description: Technical justification for this specific attenuation

cme_status.description: Whether this control is applicable to the product. Analogous to CVE status (affected/unaffected/unknown) but with CME-specific semantics: 'applicable' means the control can be deployed on this product.

cme_version.description: A version string, following the CVE 5.x version definition.

cme_product.$comment: Mirrors CVE Record Format 5.2.0 #/definitions/product. Status enum changed from affected/unaffected/unknown to applicable/not-applicable/unknown. See https://github.com/CVEProject/cve-schema

vendor.description: Name of the organization that created or maintains this product

product.description: Name of the product where this control is applicable

collectionURL.description: URL identifying a package collection (determines the meaning of packageName)

packageName.description: Name or identifier of the package as used in the package collection

packageURL.description: Package URL (PURL) for the product, without version (e.g., pkg:rpm/redhat/kernel)

cpes.description: CPE identifiers for this product (CPE 2.2 or 2.3 format)

modules.description: Affected components, features, modules, or sub-components

platforms.description: Platforms where this control applies to this product (e.g., x86_64, ARM, 64 bit). Absent means all platforms.

programFiles.description: Source code files relevant to this control on this product

programRoutines.description: Program routines relevant to this control

repo.description: URL of the source code repository

defaultStatus.description: Default applicability status for versions not listed in the versions array. Defaults to 'unknown' if omitted.

version.description: The single version being described, or the start of a version range

status.description: Applicability status for this version or range

versionType.description: The version numbering system (e.g., semver, rpm, custom, git)

lessThan.description: Non-inclusive upper limit of the version range

lessThanOrEqual.description: Inclusive upper limit of the version range

at.description: The version at which a status change occurs

status.description: The new applicability status starting at this version

changes.description: Status changes within the version range

versions.description: Version ranges where this control is applicable
